content:
# Leviticus Chapter 14 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 66r–66v | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/LEV_014_page_001.jpg`, `LEV_014_page_002.jpg` | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 99–100

---

**1** And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,

**2** "This shall be the law of the one afflicted with skin disease [tzara'at] on the day of his cleansing: he shall be brought to the priest.

**3** And the priest shall go out of the camp, and the priest shall examine him. And if the outbreak of skin disease has been healed in the one afflicted,

**4** then the priest shall command that two living clean birds and cedar wood and scarlet yarn and hyssop be taken for the one who is to be cleansed.

**5** And the priest shall command that one of the birds be slaughtered in an earthen vessel over fresh water.

**6** As for the living bird, he shall take it together with the cedar wood and the scarlet yarn and the hyssop, and he shall dip them and the living bird in the blood of the bird that was slaughtered over the fresh water.

**7** And he shall sprinkle it seven times on the one who is to be cleansed of the skin disease, and he shall pronounce him clean, and he shall set the living bird free over the open field.

**8** And the one who is to be cleansed shall wash his clothes and shave off all his hair and bathe himself in water, and he shall be clean. And afterward he may come into the camp, but he shall dwell outside his tent seven days.

**9** And on the seventh day he shall shave off all his hair: his head and his beard and his eyebrows and all his hair he shall shave off. And he shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and he shall be clean.

**10** "And on the eighth day he shall take two male lambs without blemish and one ewe lamb a year old without blemish, and three tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering, and one log of oil.

**11** And the priest who cleanses him shall set the man who is to be cleansed and these things before the LORD, at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

**12** And the priest shall take one of the male lambs and offer it as a guilt offering, along with the log of oil, and wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.

**13** And he shall slaughter the male lamb in the place where the sin offering and the burnt offering are slaughtered, in the holy place. For the guilt offering, like the sin offering, belongs to the priest; it is most holy.

**14** And the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering, and the priest shall put it on the lobe of the right ear of the one who is to be cleansed and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.

**15** Then the priest shall take some of the log of oil and pour it into the palm of his own left hand

**16** and shall dip his right finger in the oil that is in his left hand and sprinkle some of the oil with his finger seven times before the LORD.

**17** And some of the oil that remains in his hand the priest shall put on the lobe of the right ear of the one who is to be cleansed and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot, on top of the blood of the guilt offering.

**18** And the rest of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall put on the head of the one who is to be cleansed. So the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD.

**19** And the priest shall offer the sin offering and make atonement for the one who is to be cleansed from his uncleanness. And afterward he shall slaughter the burnt offering.

**20** And the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar. So the priest shall make atonement for him, and he shall be clean.

**21** "But if he is poor and cannot afford so much, then he shall take one male lamb as a guilt offering to be waved, to make atonement for him, and one tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering and a log of oil,

**22** and two turtledoves or two pigeons, whichever he can afford, one being for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering.

**23** And on the eighth day he shall bring them for his cleansing to the priest, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, before the LORD.

**24** And the priest shall take the lamb of the guilt offering and the log of oil, and the priest shall wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.

**25** And he shall slaughter the lamb of the guilt offering, and the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the lobe of the right ear of the one who is to be cleansed and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.

**26** And the priest shall pour some of the oil into the palm of his own left hand,

**27** and shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left hand seven times before the LORD.

**28** And the priest shall put some of the oil that is in his hand on the lobe of the right ear of the one who is to be cleansed and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot, in the place of the blood of the guilt offering.

**29** And the rest of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall put on the head of the one who is to be cleansed, to make atonement for him before the LORD.

**30** And he shall offer, of the turtledoves or pigeons, whichever he can afford,

**31** one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering, along with a grain offering. So the priest shall make atonement before the LORD for the one who is to be cleansed.

**32** This is the law for him who has an outbreak of skin disease, who cannot afford the offerings for his cleansing."

**33** And the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,

**34** "When you come into the land of Canaan, which I give you for a possession, and I put a mark of skin disease [nega tzara'at] in a house in the land of your possession,

**35** then he who owns the house shall come and tell the priest, 'There seems to me to be some sort of disease in my house.'

**36** Then the priest shall command that they empty the house before the priest goes in to examine the disease, lest all that is in the house be declared unclean. And afterward the priest shall go in to see the house.

**37** And he shall examine the disease. And if the disease is in the walls of the house with greenish or reddish spots, and if they appear to be deeper than the surface,

**38** then the priest shall go out of the house to the door of the house and shut up the house seven days.

**39** And the priest shall come again on the seventh day and look. If the disease has spread in the walls of the house,

**40** then the priest shall command that they take out the stones in which the disease is and throw them into an unclean place outside the city.

**41** And he shall have the inside of the house scraped all around, and they shall pour out the plaster that they scrape off outside the city in an unclean place.

**42** Then they shall take other stones and put them in place of those stones, and he shall take other plaster and plaster the house.

**43** "If the disease breaks out again in the house, after he has taken out the stones and scraped the house and plastered it,

**44** then the priest shall go and look. And if the disease has spread in the house, it is a persistent skin disease in the house; it is unclean.

**45** And he shall break down the house, its stones and timber and all the plaster of the house, and he shall carry them outside the city to an unclean place.

**46** Moreover, whoever enters the house during the days he shuts it up shall be unclean until the evening.

**47** And whoever sleeps in the house shall wash his clothes, and whoever eats in the house shall wash his clothes.

**48** "But if the priest comes and looks and the disease has not spread in the house after the house was plastered, then the priest shall pronounce the house clean, for the disease is healed.

**49** And for the cleansing of the house he shall take two birds and cedar wood and scarlet yarn and hyssop,

**50** and shall slaughter one of the birds in an earthen vessel over fresh water

**51** and shall take the cedar wood and the hyssop and the scarlet yarn, along with the living bird, and dip them in the blood of the slaughtered bird and in the fresh water and sprinkle the house seven times.

**52** Thus he shall cleanse the house with the blood of the bird and with the fresh water and with the living bird and with the cedar wood and hyssop and scarlet yarn.

**53** And he shall set the living bird free outside the city over the open field. So he shall make atonement for the house, and it shall be clean.

**54** "This is the law for any outbreak of skin disease: for a scab,

**55** and for skin disease of a garment and of a house,

**56** and for a swelling and for a scab and for a bright spot,

**57** to teach when it is unclean and when it is clean. This is the law for skin disease."

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Manuscript Observations

**Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 66r–66v:**
The text of Leviticus 14 is well preserved in the Leningrad Codex with consistent ketiv-qere readings. The chapter exhibits the characteristic repetition of priestly legislation: the ritual instructions for the healed person (vv. 1–32) are carefully structured in two parallel panels — one for the person of means (vv. 10–20) and one for the poor (vv. 21–31) — with the two-bird rite (vv. 4–7) serving as the initial cleansing ceremony shared by both. The hinge verse (v. 32) closes the first major section with a legal summary formula.

The second major section (vv. 33–53) on house tzara'at is introduced with a prospective formula ("When you come into the land of Canaan"), indicating its Canaanite-land applicability. The closing summary (vv. 54–57) employs a six-item list that recapitulates the entire two-chapter unit (chapters 13–14).

**Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 99–100:**
The LXX follows the MT closely throughout this chapter. Vaticanus presents a continuous text without major lacunae on these pages. A few characteristic LXX expansions and lexical substitutions are noted below.

---

### Key Translation Decisions

**1. "Two living clean birds" (v. 4) — shtei tzipporim chayyot tehorot:**
The Hebrew requires all three adjectives: "living," "clean" (i.e., of a ritually clean species), and the numeral "two." The LXX (δύο ὀρνίθια ζῶντα καθαρά) matches closely. "Birds" is preferred over "sparrows" (some traditions) because the species is unspecified beyond ritual cleanliness.

**2. "Cedar wood… scarlet yarn… hyssop" (v. 4):**
The three items — etz erez, sheni tola'at, and ezov — form a fixed ritual triad appearing also in Numbers 19 (red heifer) and Psalm 51:7. "Cedar wood" is literal; "scarlet yarn" renders sheni tola'at (literally "worm-scarlet," a crimson dye from the tola'at worm); "hyssop" renders ezov, an aromatic plant used for sprinkling. These are not glossed in the translation body but are identified here.

**3. "Fresh water" (vv. 5–6) — mayim chayyim:**
Literally "living water," meaning running or spring water as opposed to standing water. "Fresh water" conveys the same distinction in natural English. The LXX renders ὕδωρ ζῶν ("living water"), preserving the Hebrew idiom.

**4. "Wave offering" (vv. 12, 24) — tenufah:**
The wave offering (tenufah) is rendered consistently as "wave offering." The gesture is a ritual presentation before the LORD, possibly involving a horizontal waving motion. The LXX uses ἐπίθεμα ("contribution/addition") in some manuscripts but ἀφόρισμα in others; Vaticanus tends toward the former in this chapter.

**5. Application of blood and oil to ear, thumb, toe (vv. 14–17, 25–28):**
This three-point anointing — right earlobe, right thumb, right big toe — mirrors the ordination rite for priests in Leviticus 8:23–24. The symmetry is theologically significant: the cleansed person is reconstituted as a full member of the covenant community through a priestly-style ritual. The Hebrew is precise: "lobe of the right ear" (tenuph ozen yemino), "thumb of his right hand" (bohen yado hayemanit), "big toe of his right foot" (bohen raglo hayemanit). This precision is preserved in the translation.

**6. "One tenth of an ephah" (vv. 10, 21) — sheloshah esronim / issaron echad:**
Verse 10 specifies "three tenths of an ephah" for the person of means; verse 21 reduces this to "one tenth of an ephah" for the poor person. The contrast is deliberate and is maintained in the translation.

**7. "A log of oil" (vv. 10, 12, 15, 21, 24) — log hashemen:**
The log is a liquid measure, approximately 0.3 liters (roughly half a pint). It is preserved as "a log of oil" rather than converted to modern measures, consistent with the formal equivalence policy for units of measure.

**8. "I put a mark of skin disease in a house" (v. 34) — venatati nega tzara'at bebayit:**
The grammatical subject is the LORD himself ("I put"). This is a striking theological statement: tzara'at in houses is presented as a divine act, not merely a natural phenomenon. The verse is rendered literally to preserve this force. Some translations soften this to "there appears a mark," but the Hebrew first-person divine initiative is clear and is maintained.

**9. "Greenish or reddish spots" (v. 37) — yeraqqot o adumot:**
The Hebrew terms (from yeruq, "green/yellowish," and adom, "red") describe discoloration patterns on walls. Some translations render "greenish-yellow or reddish." The translation uses "greenish or reddish" as the most direct rendering.

**10. "Persistent skin disease" (v. 44) — tzara'at mameret:**
The adjective mameret (from the root mrd or related to mara, "to be stubborn/persistent") modifies tzara'at here uniquely. It indicates a recurrent and entrenched condition. "Persistent" captures this better than "malignant" (some older translations) or "active" (some modern ones).

**11. "So he shall make atonement for the house" (v. 53):**
The verb kipper (Pi'el of kpr, "to make atonement/cover") is applied here to a house, extending the atonement concept beyond persons. This is rendered literally and not softened to "purify" alone, though both purification and covering are in view.

**12. Summary list (vv. 54–57):**
The closing summary names six conditions — "any outbreak of skin disease," "a scab," "skin disease of a garment," "skin disease of a house," "a swelling," "a scab," "a bright spot" — forming a legal colophon. Some of the terms repeat from chapter 13. The list is rendered in full without condensation.

---

### LXX Vaticanus Variants

| Verse | MT (Hebrew) | LXX (Vaticanus) | Note |
|-------|-------------|-----------------|------|
| 4 | shtei tzipporim chayyot tehorot ("two living clean birds") | δύο ὀρνίθια ζῶντα καθαρά | Close match; LXX uses ὀρνίθια (diminutive: "little birds") |
| 5 | mayim chayyim ("fresh/living water") | ὕδωρ ζῶν | Literal Greek rendering of "living water" |
| 7 | al-pnei hasadeh ("over the open field") | ἐπὶ πρόσωπον τοῦ πεδίου | Literal; LXX uses πεδίον (plain/field) |
| 10 | log hashemen ("a log of oil") | κοτύλην ἐλαίου | LXX renders the log as a kotyle (a Greek liquid measure, ~0.27 L) |
| 12 | tenufah ("wave offering") | ἐπίθεμα | LXX departs from literal; possibly "contribution" rather than wave gesture |
| 14 | tenuph ozen yemino ("lobe of his right ear") | τοῦ ὠτὸς τοῦ δεξιοῦ | Greek matches the Hebrew body-part precision |
| 21 | issaron echad ("one tenth of an ephah") | δέκατον σεμιδάλεως | LXX rendering matches MT in proportion |
| 34 | venatati nega tzara'at ("and I will put a mark of skin disease") | καὶ δῶ ἁφὴν λέπρας | LXX smooths the divine first-person agency; "I will give a mark of leprosy" |
| 37 | yeraqqot o adumot ("greenish or reddish") | ὑποχλωρίζουσαι ἢ πυρρίζουσαι | LXX uses compound adjectives: "somewhat greenish or somewhat reddish" |
| 44 | tzara'at mameret ("persistent skin disease") | λέπρα ἐνεστηκυῖα | LXX: "established/settled leprosy" — captures the permanence |
| 53 | vekhipper al-habayit ("he shall make atonement for the house") | ἐξιλάσεται τὴν οἰκίαν | LXX uses ἐξιλάσεται (to make propitious), equivalent to kipper |

---

### Consistent Term Renderings

| Hebrew Term | LOGOS Rendering | Notes |
|-------------|-----------------|-------|
| tzara'at | skin disease [tzara'at] | Preserved with gloss on first occurrence per chapter; "skin disease" thereafter |
| nega tzara'at | mark of skin disease | Compound noun rendered literally |
| kohen | priest | Standard rendering |
| YHWH | the LORD | Small capitals as conventional |
| chattat | sin offering | Consistent with Lev 1–13 |
| olah | burnt offering | Consistent with Lev 1–13 |
| asham | guilt offering | Consistent with Lev 1–13 |
| minchah | grain offering | Consistent with Lev 1–13 |
| tenufah | wave offering | Consistent gesture-noun |
| tameh | unclean | Standard Levitical rendering |
| tahor | clean | Standard Levitical rendering |
| kipper | make atonement | Pi'el rendered with full phrase |
| mayim chayyim | fresh water | "Living water" in notes; "fresh" in text for English clarity |
| log | a log of oil | Unit preserved without modern conversion |
| etz erez | cedar wood | Literal compound |
| sheni tola'at | scarlet yarn | "Worm-scarlet" explained in notes |
| ezov | hyssop | Preserved (plant name established in Eng.) |
| bney yisrael | sons of Israel | Formal rendering throughout |
| shtei tzipporim | two birds | "Two living clean birds" in full ritual context |

---

— LOGOS
